cases.title: Hand-Drawn Layered AI Agent Architecture
cases.claim: Agent 系统的稳定性来自网关、工具编排、记忆检索、模型服务和观测链路的分层协同。
cases.scenario: Create one Huawei-style slide showing a hand-drawn layered AI agent architecture. The user enters from the top, calls three client surfaces, flows through an agent gateway, branches into tool orchestration and memory/retrieval, then reaches model/runtime services and observability on the side.
human_review_focus: Does it feel like a real hand-drawn architecture sketch?
Are layer relationships clear without becoming rigid business boxes?
Are arrows and side modules readable?
Does the diagram add visual soul beyond cards/text?

cases.title: Benchmark Improvement Bars
cases.claim: DGM agent 在不同基座模型上都显著高于 Base agent，收益不是单模型偶然现象。
cases.scenario: Create one hand-drawn grouped bar chart comparing Base agent and DGM agent across three model backbones.

visual_spec.annotation: 跨模型迁移仍保留收益，说明改进落在工作流层。

human_review_focus: Are the grouped bars immediately readable?
Is the DGM improvement visually obvious?
Are values and category labels legible?
Does it feel more alive than a plain business chart?

cases.title: Archive Evolution Tree
cases.claim: Archive 通过保留分支，让暂时低分节点也可能成为未来高分节点的踏脚石。
cases.scenario: Create one slide explaining why Archive is not cache but a search strategy. Show an agent evolution tree where low-scoring branches remain selectable and one branch eventually reaches 50%.

visual_spec.annotation: 所有节点保留非零选择概率，旧分支不会被过早丢弃。

human_review_focus: Is the branching lineage immediately understandable?
Is the final high-score node visually emphasized?
Does the visual avoid looking like a generic card list?
Are scores readable and spatially attached to nodes?

cases.title: Self-Improvement Loop
cases.claim: 自我改进不是单次 prompt 优化，而是可评测、可归档、可继续选择的闭环。
cases.scenario: Create one slide explaining an agent self-improvement loop: inspect failures, propose improvement, edit its own workflow/tools, evaluate on benchmark, archive successful variants, then select the next parent.
human_review_focus: Does the loop read as a cycle rather than a linear list?
Is the center concept clear?
Are step labels short and legible?
Does the style remain professional enough for a technical deck?

cases.title: Agent Workflow Sequence
cases.claim: 稳定的 Agent 交付链路来自可观察、可评审、可回滚的阶段化流程。
cases.scenario: Create one hand-drawn sequence diagram showing how an agent task moves from user request to planning, tool execution, review, delivery, and archive.
human_review_focus: Does it clearly read left-to-right as a process?
Is the highlighted quality gate visible?
Are notes readable without becoming cards?
Does the sequence feel hand-drawn rather than a rigid SmartArt clone?

cases.title: Visual Anchor Choice Matrix
cases.claim: 渲染路径选择取决于手绘表现力与工程可控性的平衡。
cases.scenario: Create one 2x2 matrix comparing visual anchor choices by evidence value and visual soul.
human_review_focus: Are the two axes immediately understandable?
Does each renderer sit in a plausible quadrant?
Is the highlighted recommendation clear?
Does it avoid becoming a normal text card comparison?

cases.title: Agent Tool Network
cases.claim: Agent 能力不是单点工具，而是模型、记忆、代码、浏览器和评审环节的协同网络。
cases.scenario: Create one small network diagram with Agent as the hub and five connected capability modules.
human_review_focus: Does the network show collaboration instead of hierarchy?
Are cross-links readable and not too messy?
Is the hub visually dominant?
Does the highlighted review node stand out?
